docs.title: R8: Unmanaged Position
docs.text:
interface UnmanagedPositionGate {
  name: "unmanaged_position";
  domain: "safety";
  default_effect: "block";
}

// Input
interface UnmanagedInput {
  exchange_positions: ExchangePosition[];
  projected_positions: Position[];
  active_runs: Run[];
}

// Entscheidung
function evaluateUnmanaged(input: UnmanagedInput): UnmanagedDecision {
  const unmanaged = [];

  for (const exchange of input.exchange_positions) {
    // Check: Haben wir einen Run für dieses Symbol?
    const active_run = input.active_runs.find(r => r.symbol === exchange.symbol && !r.ended_at);

    // Check: Haben wir eine Position?
    const projected = input.projected_positions.find(p => p.symbol === exchange.symbol && p.status === "open");

    if (exchange.size > 0 && (!active_run || !projected)) {
      unmanaged.push({
        symbol: exchange.symbol,
        size: exchange.size,
        side: exchange.side,
        has_run: !!active_run,
        has_projected: !!projected,
        message: `Unmanaged ${exchange.side} ${exchange.size} ${exchange.symbol} on exchange`
      });
    }
  }

  return {
    passed: unmanaged.length === 0,
    unmanaged,
    checked_at: new Date().toISOString()
  };
}

// Output
// Bei unmanaged.length > 0:
event = {
  event_type: "SAFETY_VIOLATED",
  payload: {
    gate: "unmanaged_position",
    severity: "block",
    unmanaged: decision.unmanaged,
    message: `${decision.unmanaged.length} unmanaged position(s) detected - PAUSING NOW`
  }
};

docs.title: R9: Report Failure
docs.text:
interface ReportGate {
  name: "report_failure";
  domain: "observability";
  default_effect: "warn";  // NIEMALS block
}

// Input
interface ReportInput {
  last_report_attempt_at: string | null;
  last_successful_report_at: string | null;
  consecutive_failures: number;
}

// Entscheidung
function evaluateReport(input: ReportInput): ReportDecision {
  if (input.consecutive_failures >= 3) {
    return {
      passed: false,
      severity: "warn",  // EXPLIZIT warn, niemals block
      consecutive_failures: input.consecutive_failures,
      message: `Report failed ${input.consecutive_failures} times consecutively`
    };
  }

  return { passed: true };
}

// Output
// Bei passed === false:
event = {
  event_type: "OBSERVABILITY_WARN",
  payload: {
    gate: "report",
    severity: "warn",  // NICHT "block"
    consecutive_failures: decision.consecutive_failures,
    message: decision.message,
    note: "This is an OBSERVABILITY warning - trading continues"
  }
};

docs.title: 4.1 Safety-Events → Effekte
docs.text: Event Feld Wert Effekt SAFETY_VIOLATED severity "block" Trading pause, Alert, Manual review required SAFETY_VIOLATED severity "warn" Log entry, Discord warn, Trading continues SAFETY_RESOLVED gate <gate_name> Resume möglich (nach manual check)

docs.title: 4.2 Observability-Events → Effekte
docs.text:
Event Feld Wert Effekt OBSERVABILITY_WARN severity "warn" Log entry, Discord warn, Trading continues OBSERVABILITY_WARN severity "info" Log entry only OBSERVABILITY_RESOLVED gate <gate_name> Status zurücksetzen

WICHTIG: Observability-Events haben keinen severity: "block". Falls ein Code-Pfad das versucht, wird es zur Laufzeit in "warn" konvertiert.

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Klare Domain-Trennung: Safety blockt, Observability warnt
  • Jedes Gate hat explizite Input/Output-Spezifikation
  • Testbar: jedes Gate isoliert testbar
  • Keine Überraschungen: Discord-Ausfall blockiert nie

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Mehr Code (jedes Gate ist separate Funktion)
  • Mehr Events (jedes Gate erzeugt eigenes Event)
  • Komplexität bei Multi-Gate-Validierung

docs.title: Migration
docs.text:
• Legacy risk_check.js wird ersetzt durch risk_engine.js
  • Einzel-Gates können schrittweise migriert werden

docs.title: ADR-005: Three-Layer Architecture + Sentiment Risk Layer
docs.text: Status: APPROVED Updated: 2026-04-20

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: The system uses a Three-Layer Architecture with a Sentiment Risk Layer for V2+.

docs.title: Layer 1: Foundry (Backtesting)
docs.text:
MACD Momentum + ADX+EMA Regime Filter — FROZEN during paper trading.

  • Entry: macd_hist > 0 AND close > ema_50 AND ema_50 > ema_200 AND adx_14 > 20
  • Exit: Trailing Stop 2%, SL 2.5%, Max Hold 48 Bars
  • Gate: CL≤12, DD≤20%, PF≥1.05, Return≥1%, Sharpe≥0.1, Trades≥20
  • Validation: 75% Pass Rate (12/16), avg DD 14.1%, avg CL 6.5

docs.title: Layer 2: Executor (Live Trading)
docs.text:
Paper Engine V1 — running on Hyperliquid Testnet since 2026-04-20.

  • 7 Modules: data_feed, signal_generator, state_manager, risk_guard, paper_engine, discord_reporter, command_listener
  • 83 Tests (75 Unit + 7 E2E + 1 Regression), 100% Pass
  • 5 Bug Audits completed (17 bugs found & fixed)
  • Guard States: RUNNING → SOFT_PAUSE → STOP_NEW → KILL_SWITCH → COOLDOWN → RUNNING
  • Kill-Switches: CL≥5, DD>20%, DailyLoss>5%
  • Leverage Tiers (ADR-007): BTC/ETH 1.8x, SOL/DOGE/ADA 1.5x, AVAX 1.0x

docs.title: Layer 3: Monitor (Planned)
docs.text: Dashboard, equity curve per bar, daily reports, alerting.

docs.title: Sentiment/Macro Risk Layer (V2+)
docs.text:
KI als Signalgeber = YES, KI als Richter = NO

Aspect Decision Role Signal Filter, nicht Trade-Entscheidung Score 0-100 (0=bearish, 100=bullish) Format JSON-Mode für deterministische Extraktion Fail-safe Bei Fehler → ignorieren (nicht handeln ist sicherer) Direction Nur Downsizing, nie Upsizing Priority V2 High für 1h Timeframe

docs.title: V2 Sentiment Sources (Priority)
docs.text:
1. Crypto-specific news (CoinDesk, CryptoSlate) — High Priority
  2. Macro indicators (Fed, CPI, DXY) — Medium Priority
  3. Social sentiment (Fear&Greed Index, Reddit) — Low Priority
  4. On-chain data (Exchange Netflow 7-14d) — Medium Priority

docs.title: V2 Alpha Stack (Priority)
docs.text:
1. Asset-Ranking (ROC-basiert) — Hoch
  2. ADX-based Position Sizing — Hoch
  3. 2x Leverage für Blue Chips — Mittel
  4. Limit-Orders statt Market — Mittel
  5. HTF-Alignment — Niedrig
  6. ~~ATR-Stops~~ — ABGELEHNT
  7. ~~Kelly Criterion~~ — ABGELEHNT

docs.title: Explicitly Rejected
docs.text:
• ATR-based stops (no improvement over baseline)
  • Kelly criterion (unstable win-rate in crypto)
  • 3x leverage (DD 29-51%, constant KILL triggers)
  • Dynamic auto-derate (too error-prone for V1)
  • KI als Richter (AI decides trade direction)

docs.title: ADR-006: Paper/Forward Trading Setup — Executor V1
docs.text: Date: 2026-04-19 (Updated: 2026-04-20) Status: Accepted Authors: Dave + Pecz

docs.title: Context
docs.text: Foundry V1 baseline (MACD Momentum + ADX+EMA) is committed as gold standard with 50% pass rate under unchanged gates. No further filter tests. Next step: prove the strategy works under real-time conditions with deterministic execution and kill-switches.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Build Executor V1 for paper/forward trading on Hyperliquid testnet with live data feed, simulated order flow, logging, Discord reporting, and runtime kill-switches.

docs.title: 1. Data Feed (Hyperliquid WebSocket)
docs.text:
• Connect to wss://api.hyperliquid.xyz/ws (mainnet for prices)
  • Subscribe to 1h candles for BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT (start with 2 assets)
  • Reconnect on disconnect with exponential backoff
  • Buffer last 200 candles for indicator calculation

docs.title: 2. Signal Generator (Deterministic)
docs.text:
• Exact same logic as backtest: macd_hist > 0 AND close > ema_50 AND ema_50 > ema_200 AND adx_14 > 20
  • Calculated on every new 1h candle close
  • No LLM, no ambiguity — pure formula
  • Output: SIGNAL_LONG or SIGNAL_FLAT

docs.title: 3. Position Manager (Simulated)
docs.text:
• Track position state: NO_POSITION, IN_LONG, COOLDOWN
  • Simulated order fills at close price + slippage (1bp)
  • Trailing stop: 2.0%, Stop loss: 2.5%, Max hold: 48 bars
  • Position size: 100% of capital (1x leverage)
  • Log every entry, exit, stop, timeout as trade event

docs.title: 4. Risk Guard (Kill-Switch Engine)
docs.text: Guard Threshold Action Daily Loss > 5% of starting equity STOP_NEW_ENTRIES for 24h Max Drawdown > 20% from peak KILL_SWITCH: close all, stop trading Max Open Positions > 1 Reject new entry signal Consecutive Losses ≥ 5 in a row SOFT_PAUSE: 24h cooldown Drift Monitor Live metrics > 2σ from backtest expectations ALERT + reduce position to 50%

docs.title: 5. Runtime Guards (Explicit States)
docs.text:
RUNNING     → Normal trading, all guards clear
SOFT_PAUSE  → No new entries for 24h, existing positions managed normally
STOP_NEW    → No new entries, existing positions managed (more restrictive than SOFT_PAUSE)
KILL_SWITCH → All positions closed immediately, no new trading until manual reset
COOLDOWN    → Mandatory 24h wait after KILL_SWITCH before resuming

State transitions: - RUNNING → SOFT_PAUSE: 5+ consecutive losses - RUNNING → STOP_NEW: Daily loss > 5% - RUNNING → KILL_SWITCH: Drawdown > 20% OR manual trigger - SOFT_PAUSE → RUNNING: 24h elapsed, no losses in cooldown - KILL_SWITCH → COOLDOWN: Automatic after position close - COOLDOWN → RUNNING: 24h elapsed + manual confirmation via Discord - Any → KILL_SWITCH: Manual Discord command !kill

docs.title: 6. Discord Reporter
docs.text:
• Trade execution: Entry/exit with PnL
  • Hourly status: Position state, unrealized PnL, equity curve
  • Guard triggers: Kill-switch, pause, cooldown events
  • Daily summary: Total PnL, win rate, drawdown, Sharpe estimate
  • Drift alerts: Live metrics deviating from backtest expectations

docs.title: 7. State Manager (SQLite)
docs.text:
• Persistent state: position, equity, guard states, trade history
  • Recoverable after crash/restart
  • Trade log as JSONL for post-analysis

docs.title: 8. Trade Log (JSONL)
docs.text:
{
  "timestamp": "2026-04-19T14:00:00Z",
  "event": "ENTRY",
  "asset": "BTCUSDT",
  "side": "LONG",
  "price": 85432.50,
  "size": 0.00117,
  "equity": 100.00,
  "signal": "macd_hist>0, close>ema50, ema50>ema200, adx14>20",
  "guard_state": "RUNNING"
}

docs.title: Two-Phase Validation (Updated 2026-04-20)
docs.text:
Original plan: 30-day paper trading only. Revised to 14+14 after Dave's review:

"14 Tage Paper, dann 14 Tage echte Testnet-Orders — die API-Integration ist die echte Lücke."

Rationale: Paper trading proves the engine runs, not that the strategy works (backtest already did that). The untested gap is API integration — 14 days of real testnet orders brings more value than 16 more days of watching the paper motor run.

docs.title: Phase 1: Paper Trading (14 days)
docs.text: Criterion Threshold Notes Min trades ≥ 10 Conservative strategy, ~1-2 trades/asset/month Max drawdown ≤ 25% 5% buffer over backtest 20% gate Accounting invariant ✅ equity = initial - fees + sum(net_pnl) holds Execution quality ≥ 95% of signals evaluated No missed candle closes Kill-switch response ≤ 60s from trigger to action Run duration ≥ 14 days Engine stability + signal validation Uptime ≥ 99% during trading hours

docs.title: Phase 2: Testnet API Trading (14 days)
docs.text: Criterion Threshold Notes Min orders placed ≥ 5 Real API calls to Hyperliquid testnet API error rate 0% No unhandled exceptions in order flow Position visible ✅ Trades appear on app.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz Kill-switch works ✅ Closes position via API on !kill Execution quality ≥ 95% Signals → Orders within 60s Run duration ≥ 14 days API stability + edge case handling

docs.title: Gate Between Phases
docs.text: Phase 2 only starts if Phase 1 passes all criteria. If Phase 1 fails: - Fix bugs, restart 14-day clock - If fundamental strategy failure → back to Foundry

docs.title: Total Timeline
docs.text: Week Phase Focus 1-2 Paper Engine stability, signal validation, accounting 3-4 Testnet API Order placement, API edge cases, real execution 5 Decision Go/No-Go for mainnet

docs.title: Original Success Criteria (superseded)
docs.text: Criterion Threshold Notes Min trades ≥ 30 Statistical relevance Max drawdown ≤ 25% 5% buffer over backtest 20% gate Win rate deviation ≤ 10pp from backtest e.g. 40%±10 Avg trade PnL deviation ≤ 2σ from backtest mean Execution quality ≥ 95% of signals executed Slippage/timeout tracking Uptime ≥ 99% during trading hours Kill-switch response ≤ 60s from trigger to action Run duration ≥ 30 days Minimum observation period

docs.title: Implementation Order (completed)
docs.text:
1. ✅ Data feed + Signal generator + State manager
  2. ✅ Position manager + Risk guard + Trade logger
  3. ✅ Discord reporter + Command listener
  4. ✅ Integration test + 82 automated tests (75 unit + 7 E2E)
  5. 🔄 Phase 1: Paper trading (14 days, started 2026-04-20)
  6. ⬜ Phase 2: Testnet API trading (14 days, requires Phase 1 pass)

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
• Positive: Real-time validation of strategy before risking capital
  • Positive: Kill-switches ensure we never lose more than expected
  • Positive: Two-phase approach validates both engine AND API
  • Positive: Faster path to mainnet decision (~5 weeks vs 8+ weeks)
  • Negative: 10-trade threshold is low — limited statistical confidence
  • Negative: Simulated execution (Phase 1) may differ from real (slippage, latency)
  • Negative: 14 days may miss regime changes visible in longer runs

docs.title: Out of Scope
docs.text:
• Real money trading (only after Phase 2 passes all criteria)
  • Multiple strategies simultaneously (V2+)
  • Short positions (V1 is long-only)
  • News/macro risk layer (V2+, see ADR-005)
  • Limit orders, TWAP, or advanced execution (V2+)

docs.title: ADR-007: Leverage Tiers & Alpha Stack V2
docs.text: Status: Approved (2026-04-19) Context: Paper Trading setup, cost coverage required Decision: Asset-specific static leverage tiers + Alpha Stack V2 roadmap

docs.title: Leverage Tiers (Static)
docs.text:
Tier Leverage Assets DD Range Rationale 1 1.8x BTC, ETH 18.3-18.5% Low volatility, deepest liquidity 2 1.5x SOL, DOGE, ADA 17.9-19.9% Medium volatility 3 1.0x AVAX 18.3% High volatility, conservative

Why static, not dynamic: Dynamic auto-derate (reduce leverage on guard trigger) adds state complexity to the State Manager. For V1, static tiers are robust, predictable, and sufficient. Dynamic leverage is a V2+ consideration.

Why not 3x: DD at 3x = 29-51%. KILL switch fires constantly. Mathematically untenable.

Why 1.8x not 2x for Tier 1: BTC/ETH at 2x → DD = 20.2-20.4% → exactly at KILL threshold. 1.8x gives 18.3-18.5% DD — 1.5pp buffer to KILL. Still delivers +40% return.

docs.title: ADR-008: Crash & Uptime Strategy (Phase 2)
docs.text: Status: Proposed Date: 2026-04-21 Context: Phase 2 — Testnet API Orders (echte Orders auf Testnet)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Wenn das System offline ist, laufen offene Positionen ungeschützt. Kein SL, kein Trailing, keine Signal-Generierung. In Phase 1 (Paper Mode) verschmerzbar. In Phase 2 (echte API Orders) katastrophal.

docs.title: Failure Scenarios
docs.text: # Szenario Wahrscheinlichkeit Max Downtime Risiko 1 Paper Engine crasht, VPS läuft Mittel 60 Min (Watchdog) Position unmanaged 2 Docker/OpenClaw crasht, VPS läuft Niedrig Bis manueller Restart Position unmanaged 3 VPS down (Hostinger Wartung/HW-Fehler) Sehr niedrig 5-120 Min Position unmanaged, kein Restart 4 VPS unreachable (Netzwerk/DDoS) Sehr niedrig Variabel Position unmanaged, kein Restart 5 Hyperliquid API down Sehr niedrig 5-30 Min Keine Orders möglich, Position bleibt

docs.title: Layer 1: Heartbeat File (intern)
docs.text:
Löst: Szenario 1 — Engine crasht, Rest läuft

  • Paper Engine schreibt Timestamp alle 30s nach heartbeat.json
  • Watchdog prüft alle 60s: ist heartbeat.json jünger als 90s?
  • Nein → Engine als "stale" markiert → Auto-Restart + Discord Alert
  • Reduziert Erkennungszeit: 60 Min → 1-2 Min
# In paper_engine.py — main loop
HEARTBEAT_PATH = "heartbeat.json"
# Alle 30s:
json.dump({"ts": time.time(), "pid": os.getpid(), "positions": len(open_positions)}, 
          open(HEARTBEAT_PATH, "w"))

# In watchdog_v2.py — additional check
hb = json.load(open("heartbeat.json"))
if time.time() - hb["ts"] > 90:
    # Engine stale → escalate

docs.title: Layer 2: Position Recovery (intern)
docs.text:
Löst: Szenarien 1+2 — Nach Restart, Positionen sicher wieder aufnehmen

  • Nach jedem Restart: state.db laden, offene Positionen identifizieren
  • Sofort SL/Trailing prüfen gegen aktuelle Preise
  • Wenn SL überschritten → Not-Close sofort
  • Wenn Position > max_hold Bars alt → Not-Close
  • Recovery-Log an Discord
def recover_positions(engine):
    for pos in engine.state_manager.get_open_positions():
        current_price = engine.data_feed.get_mid_price(pos.symbol)
        # SL Check
        if pos.side == "LONG" and current_price <= pos.stop_loss:
            engine.close_position(pos, reason="RECOVERY_SL")
        # Max Hold Check
        if (datetime.utcnow() - pos.opened_at).total_seconds() > MAX_HOLD_SECONDS:
            engine.close_position(pos, reason="RECOVERY_MAX_HOLD")

docs.title: Layer 3: External Uptime Monitor (extern)
docs.text:
Löst: Szenarien 3+4 — VPS down oder unreachable

  • UptimeRobot (gratis Plan): HTTP-Ping auf VPS-IP alle 5 Min
  • Bei Ausfall: Discord-Webhook an #system Channel
  • Dave bekommt Push-Notification auf Handy
  • Erkennungszeit: max 5 Min

Setup: 1. UptimeRobot Account → Monitor → HTTP(s) → VPS-IP:Port 2. Alert Contact → Discord Webhook (#system Channel) 3. Fallback: E-Mail an Dave

Erweitert (optional): - Healthcheck-Endpoint in Paper Engine: /health → {"status":"ok","pid":123,"positions":1,"last_heartbeat":...} - UptimeRobot pingt /health statt nur TCP-Connect

docs.title: Layer 4: Kill-Switch Fallback (Hyperliquid)
docs.text:
Löst: Alle Szenarien — Letzter Ausweg

  • Hyperliquid API-Wallet Timeout: Leider kein natives Auto-Cancel wie bei CEX (Bybit/Bybit haben das)
  • Workaround: Pre-signed Cancel-Order (nicht verfügbar auf Hyperliquid)
  • Realistischer Fallback: Manuelles Schließen via Hyperliquid UI oder Mobile App
  • Dave hat Main-Wallet Access → kann Positionen manuell schließen

V2-Ziel: Sentiment-Kill-Switch als automatischer Not-Aus (ADR-005)

docs.title: Escalation Ladder
docs.text: Zeit seit Ausfall Aktion Automatisch? 0-90s Heartbeat stale erkannt ✅ Watchdog 90s-5min Auto-Restart + Discord Alert ✅ Watchdog V2 5-15min UptimeRobot Alert (VPS down) ✅ External 15-30min Dave muss manuell intervenieren ❌ Manuell 30min+ Circuit Breaker → keine neuen Trades bis bestätigt ✅ Watchdog V2

docs.text:

  • Watchdog V2 prüft heartbeat.json (zusätzlich zu PID-Check)
  • Position Recovery in paper_engine.py Restart-Flow eingebaut
  • UptimeRobot Account eingerichtet + Discord Webhook
  • Healthcheck-Endpoint oder VPS-IP-Monitor aktiv
  • Dave hat Hyperliquid Testnet UI Access für manuelle Schließung
  • Escalation Ladder in watchdog_v2.py dokumentiert
  • Test: Engine killen → Watchdog erkennt + restartet → Position recovered

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Parken bis Phase 2. In Phase 1 (Paper Mode) ist das Risiko rein virtuell. Die Strategie wird vor Phase 2-Start implementiert und getestet.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
• Phase 2 Start blockiert bis alle 8 Checklist-Items erledigt
  • UptimeRobot = externe Abhängigkeit (gratis, aber Drittanbieter)
  • Manuelle Intervention immer noch möglich (Dave + Main Wallet)

docs.text: Status: Accepted Date: 2026-04-27 Context: V2 Strategy benötigt neue Datenquellen (Regime, Orderflow, Relative Stärke, Sentiment). Wir handeln auf Hyperliquid — welche Daten sind verfügbar?

docs.title: Nicht verfügbare (direkt)
docs.text: Daten Problem Workaround 1h/15m/5m/1m Candles API liefert 0 Bars Aus Parquet-Dateien oder WS-Aggregation Open Interest 422 Error Funding Rate als Proxy; evtl. WebSocket Liquidations 422 Error Funding Rate als Proxy Deep Orderbook (>20) Nur 20 Levels Reicht für Imbalance-Ratio Historische Trades Nur letzte 10 WebSocket sammeln (V2 Stufe 2)

docs.title: Entscheidung
docs.text:
• Stufe 1 (Regime + Sniper): Alle benötigten Daten verfügbar. Kein Plan B nötig.
  • 1h Candles: Bereits als Parquet vorhanden (Paper Engine nutzt sie). Kein API-Problem.
  • Open Interest / Liquidations: Für Stufe 2. Funding Rate als Proxy für Stufe 1.
  • Deep Orderbook: 20 Levels reichen für Imbalance-Ratio (V2 Stufe 1).

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
• V2 Stufe 1 kann mit vorhandenen Daten gebaut werden
  • 1h-Daten kommen aus Parquet, nicht aus API-Candles
  • Open Interest muss für Stufe 2 über WebSocket oder alternative API-Route erschlossen werden
  • Testnet hat dieselben Endpoints aber dünnere Daten — V2-Backtests müssen Mainnet-Daten nutzen

docs.title: ADR-010: V2 Strategy — Der Oktopus
docs.text: Status: Proposed Date: 2026-04-28 Context: Post Paper Trading — V2 Strategy Design

docs.title: Decision
docs.text:
V2 heißt Oktopus. 8 Arme (6 Assets + Regime + Sentiment), 3 Herzen (Strategisch, Operativ, Physisch), knochenlos (Regime-adaptiv).

Vollständiges Design: v2-design.md

docs.title: Stufe 1 Scope (7 Features)
docs.text: # Feature Beweis 1 Regime-Score (ADX 45% + Slope 30% + Vol 25%) Backtest: Sharpe > V1 2 Sniper (Regime > 70, 5x Hebel, Trail 2.5%) Backtest + Paper 3 DD-Scaling (10/15/20%) + Global Equity Stop (1h-DD > 8%) Paper DD < 25% 4 Sentiment = Funding Rate (DIY, nicht AIXBT bezahlt) Backtest Invertierung 5 Slippage Tax (SLIPPAGE_BPS) Trivial 6 Decision Logging (SKIP-Events) Telemetrie 7 Exchange-seitige Stop-Loss (Dead Man's Switch) Infrastruktur

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
1. AIXBT Grounding = kostenlos nutzen (1h-Polling für Macro/Crypto-Regime)
  2. Sentiment = DIY (Funding Rate + Fear&Greed Index, kein AIXBT Data-Plan)
  3. Momentum-Clustering = skip für V2-Start, erst nach Profitabilität evaluieren
  4. Starrer Trail in Stufe 1 (2.5% Sniper, 2.0% V1), kinetischer Trail in Stufe 2
  5. Market-Orders in Stufe 1 (Fill-Rate > Savings bei 100€ Kapital)
  6. Korrelations-Filter Stufe 2 (Rolling 20-Bar, r>0.7 blockt Entry)
  7. Kein Re-Entry nach Sniper-Exit — Score muss <70 fallen und wieder >70 steigen

docs.title: Was NICHT in Stufe 1
docs.text:
• Kinetischer Trail → Stufe 2
  • Dynamic Cooldown → Stufe 2 (starr 24h reicht)
  • Volatility-Parität → Stufe 2
  • SHORT → Stufe 2
  • Gezeiten-Blocker → Stufe 2
  • News Sentiment → Stufe 3

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
• V1 bleibt FROZEN während Paper Trading (14+14 Tage)
  • V2-Entwicklung startet nach Foundry Review (~02.05.)
  • Stufe 1 muss Backtest + Paper beweisen bevor Stufe 2
  • Oktopus-Test: Jedes neue Feature muss die Frage "Braucht der Oktopus einen 9. Arm?" bestehen

docs.title: ADR-011: Foundry V9 — Oktopus Redesign
docs.text: Status: Accepted · 2026-04-29 Supersedes: V8 Oktopus Architecture

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
After 150+ strategies and 8 evolution versions, zero strategies pass the new WF-Gate V8.1. The old gate rewarded "not losing" over "making money":

  • +0.001% counted same as +5% — profitable_ratio was binary
  • 0-1 trades/window passed — statistical noise treated as edge
  • All 4 "champions" demoted — V33 (0.4 trades/window), VolSqueeze (OOS -0.55%), V17 (OOS -0.15%)

docs.title: Key Lessons
docs.text: # Lesson Evidence 1 MR works only on DOGE/ADA/AVAX Consistent 0/6 on BTC/ETH across all MR strategies 2 Trailing Stop 2-3% never fires 80-91% signal_exit, 1-5% trailing_stop 3 Entries too restrictive 0.4-1.3 trades/window (target: 5+) 4 Non-MR arms = 0 WF-passed TREND, MOM, REGIME, 4H all failed 5 IS-Score overfits 150x V17 IS=4.88 but OOS=-0.15% 6 Standard indicators have no edge on 1h-crypto 150+ combinations, 0 real edge

docs.title: V9 Arms (Redesigned)
docs.text: Arm Name Target Entry Style Exit 1 MR-ALT DOGE/ADA/AVAX only Loose MR (RSI<35, BB without filter) Signal-Reversal 2 MR-RELAXED All 6 Very loose (RSI<40, BB_Lower only) Signal-Reversal 3 TREND-REGIME All 6 Only in trending regime (ADX>20 + EMA slope) Signal-Reversal 4 SIGNAL-EXIT All 6 Paired entry+exit (RSI<30 → RSI>50) Explicit signal 5 VOL-BOOSTED All 6 Volume + money flow (MFI, CMF, OBV) Signal-Reversal 6 VOLATILITY-BREAK All 6 Volatility Squeeze + Breakout (BB_Width, ATR, Keltner) Signal-Reversal

docs.title: WF-Gate V8.1 (New Scoring)
docs.text:
Component Weight Range Floor OOS Return 40% 0..+2% 0 (negative = fail) Profitable Ratio 30% 0..1 ≥2/3 target assets Trade Floor 30% 3..50 trades/window <3 = fail

PASS requires ALL of: - robustness ≥ 40 - OOS > 0 on target assets - ≥ 3 trades/asset/window average - ≥ 2/3 target assets profitable (MR) or ≥ 3/6 (non-MR)

docs.title: Composite Fitness V9
docs.text: Component Weight Range Floor OOS Return 30% 0..+2% 0 (negative = 0) Target-Asset Profitable 25% per target group — Trade Quality 20% 3..50 <3 = 0 WF Robustness 15% 0..100 — Drawdown 10% 0..20% >20% = 0

docs.title: Mutation Guards
docs.text:
1. Only mutate HOF members — no random base
  2. Entry-only mutations — exit is Signal-Reversal (fixed per arm)
  3. Base must have ≥3 trades/asset/window before mutation
  4. MR max 40% of HOF — enforced by quota
  5. Temperature per arm — MR=0.3-0.4, TREND/CROSS=0.5-0.6

docs.title: Exit Overhaul
docs.text:
• Trailing Stop REMOVED — never fires at 2-3% on 1h
  • Signal-Reversal-Exit: explicit exit condition (RSI>50, close>bb_mid, close>ema_20)
  • Stop Loss stays (3-4%) and Max Hold stays (18-24 bars)
  • Arm 4 (SIGNAL-EXIT): LLM generates both entry AND exit as a pair

docs.title: IS Pre-Filter
docs.text:
• Strategies with <3 trades/asset/window skip WF entirely
  • Saves ~80% compute time on noise strategies

docs.title: HOF
docs.text:
• Starts EMPTY — all V8 entries failed V8.1 gate
  • Per-asset OOS breakdown stored in entries
  • Signal-exit vs stop-loss exit ratios tracked

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
Positive: - Eliminates "not losing" false champions - Asset-specific MR targets volatile alts where it works - Signal-Reversal exits match actual exit behavior - Pre-filter saves compute on noise strategies

Negative: - May still find 0 strategies — 1h standard indicators may simply have no edge - Asset-specific targets make comparison harder - Signal-Reversal needs explicit exit conditions in DSL

Neutral: - V8 code preserved as reference - V9 is backward-compatible with backtest engine (signal_exit already dominant)

docs.title: 4.2 Parser Robustness
docs.text:
Multi-Format Support: 1. OpenAI Format: choices[0].message.content 2. Ollama Direct: message.content 3. Alternative: response

JSON Extraction Strategy: 1. Code-Block mit json tag: ```json {...} ``` 2. Code-Block ohne tag: ```{...} ``` 3. Raw JSON: first { to last } 4. Whole response as JSON

Error Handling: - API errors → {"error": "HTTP 401: ..."} - Parse errors → {"parse_error": true, "raw_preview": "..."} - No silent failures

docs.title: 4.3 Fallback Analysis
docs.text:
Trigger: API unavailable / no key configured Behavior: Heuristic evaluation based on scorecard metrics Output: Valid AnalystReport with confidence 0.5, verdict TWEAK/FAIL/PASS based on thresholds

Thresholds: - PASS: trade_count ≥30, return >5%, drawdown >-20%, PF>1.3, WR>45%, exp>0 - FAIL: trade_count <10 or return <-5% - TWEAK: everything else
